page: Microsoft Azure L8aos_v4 is a storage and memory-optimized server instance within the Laosv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 2 threads per core using x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz and is equipped with 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It bundles a substantial 5760 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, making it highly suitable for data-intensive workloads. No GPUs are included in this configuration, and it does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25 percent. Text processing performance is also strong. However, multi-core CPU performance, memory write bandwidth, and software compilation fall into the average tier. A notable bottleneck is the large-block read-only memory bandwidth, which performs in the bottom 25 percent, along with medium-model LLM prompt processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L8aos_v4 offers a high density of local storage and memory relative to its 8 vCPU compute allocation, making it a resource-dense option for workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. It is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, text processing pipelines, and storage-heavy applications that benefit from high local capacity and strong single-thread execution.
description: Microsoft Azure L8aos_v4 is an x86_64 storage and memory-optimized server from the Laosv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a massive 5760 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance delivers strong single-core CPU and text processing performance, though its multi-core metrics and large-block memory read bandwidth are more moderate. Lacking a GPU and complimentary public IPv4 addresses, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-heavy and memory-intensive workloads. It is highly suited for database operations, caching layers, and text processing applications that require substantial local storage capacity alongside strong single-threaded execution.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L8aos_v4 is an 8-vCPU server featuring an AMD EPYC processor, 64 GB of RAM, and 5760 GB of local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for database and storage workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L8aos_v4 is an 8-vCPU server with 64 GB RAM and 5760 GB local storage, optimized for databases and storage-heavy workloads.
tagline: A resource-dense server combining high-capacity local storage and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 8 vCPUs and 3.7 GHz clock speed
64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
5760 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Best suited for database hosting, caching, and storage-heavy workloads
